steps.title: Find the line from two table points
steps.content: The table gives points such as $\left(2,-\frac{183}{10}\right)$ and $\left(4,-\frac{171}{10}\right)$. Since the relationship is linear, use those points to find the slope:$$m=\frac{-\frac{171}{10}-\left(-\frac{183}{10}\right)}{4-2}=\frac{\frac{12}{10}}{2}=\frac{3}{5}.$$

steps.title: Read the intercept from the line
steps.content: Use $(2,-\frac{183}{10})$ in $y=\frac{3}{5}x+b$:$$-\frac{183}{10}=\frac{3}{5}(2)+b$$ $$-\frac{183}{10}=\frac{12}{10}+b\Rightarrow b=-\frac{195}{10}=-\frac{39}{2}$$So the line is $y=\frac{3}{5}x-\frac{39}{2}$. The $x$-intercept occurs when $y=0$.
